evals.prompt:
Hey, can you review this page component I wrote for our savings reporting feature? I want to make sure it follows our codebase conventions. Here's the file:

[contents of eval1-savings-page.tsx]
evals.expected_output: Review should flag: unnecessary useMemo/useCallback (React 19 compiler handles this), custom SavingsMetricCard using inline styles instead of existing Metric/Card components, custom CustomBadge duplicating components/ui/badge.tsx, custom CustomTooltip duplicating components/ui/tooltip/, local helper components defined BEFORE the main export, and inline style objects instead of Tailwind classes.
expectations: Flags useMemo as unnecessary given React 19 with React Compiler
Flags useCallback as unnecessary given React 19 with React Compiler
Identifies CustomBadge as duplicating the existing badge.tsx component in components/ui/
Identifies CustomTooltip as duplicating the existing tooltip component in components/ui/tooltip/
Identifies SavingsMetricCard using inline styles instead of Tailwind classes
Flags that local components (SavingsHeader, SavingsMetricCard, etc.) are defined before the main exported component instead of after it
Suggests using existing Metric or Card components for the metric cards
Review follows the Summary / Issues / Suggestions / Good patterns structure

evals.prompt:
I just wrote this AssetDetailCard component. It's in components/ at the top level. Can you take a look and tell me if anything is off?

[contents of eval2-asset-detail-card.tsx]
evals.expected_output: Review should flag: wrong directory placement (feature-specific component in shared components/ instead of features/assets/components/), use of `any` types for unit objects, custom LoadingSkeleton when Skeleton component exists in components/ui/skeleton/, inline SVG ChevronIcon instead of using lucide-react icons, and custom table markup instead of using the Table component from components/ui/table/. Should praise: local components placed at end of file, good use of existing Card components.
expectations: Flags that the component is in components/ but should be in features/assets/components/ since it's asset-specific
Identifies the use of `any` type annotations and suggests proper typing
Notes the custom LoadingSkeleton could use the existing Skeleton component from components/ui/skeleton/
Notes the custom table markup could use the Table component from components/ui/table/
Acknowledges good patterns like local components being placed at the end of the file
Review follows the Summary / Issues / Suggestions / Good patterns structure

evals.prompt:
Can you review this nomination table component? It's a core piece of our nomination feature. I'm especially curious if there are any structural or performance things I should worry about.

[contents of eval3-nomination-feature.tsx]
evals.expected_output: Review should flag: heavy frontend computation (hourly aggregates should be backend), custom SelectDropdown duplicating components/ui/select.tsx, custom NumberInput duplicating components/ui/inputs, custom ActionButton duplicating components/ui/button/, excessive useMemo/useCallback/memo usage, inline styles on summary stats cards, and local components (SelectDropdown, NumberInput, ActionButton) defined before the main export.
expectations: Flags the hourlyAggregates computation as something that should happen on the backend, not the frontend
Flags excessive useMemo/useCallback usage as unnecessary with React Compiler
Identifies React.memo on NominationRow as likely unnecessary with React Compiler
Identifies SelectDropdown as duplicating the existing Select component in components/ui/
Identifies ActionButton as duplicating the existing Button component in components/ui/button/
Identifies inline style objects on the summary stat cards instead of Tailwind classes
Flags that local components (SelectDropdown, NumberInput, ActionButton) are defined before the main exported component
Review follows the Summary / Issues / Suggestions / Good patterns structure
